architecturalImpact.summary: Additive-only change following established patterns. Creates 4 new files and modifies 3 existing files with minimal coupling. No existing interfaces, systems, or render paths are altered.

strengths: Reuses RIBBONFIELD_ATTRIBUTES layout — identical to existing ribbon system, no new attribute shapes
Uses noise3d.glsl prepend pattern consistent with all 10 existing vertex shaders
Quality profile extension follows same per-system maxPoints pattern (maxRibbonPoints → maxFlowRibbonPoints)
ModeManager registration is purely additive — no changes to existing mode order or lifecycle
ShaderMaterial config (AdditiveBlending, transparent, depthWrite:false) matches all existing point-based systems
Fragment shader fog model reuses existing uFogNear/uFogFar uniform pair

deviations.item: New uFlowScale uniform not in COMMON_UNIFORMS
deviations.recommendation: Consider using uDisplacementScale (already in COMMON_UNIFORMS with default 0.5) instead of adding uFlowScale. This avoids diverging the uniform set. If uFlowScale semantics are truly distinct, add it to COMMON_UNIFORMS rather than as a one-off.

deviations.item: Streamline-based initial geometry is a novel generation strategy
deviations.recommendation: This is appropriate — the plan correctly identifies that helical bands (ribbonField) vs curl3-advected streamlines (flowRibbonField) are what create distinct visual character. No concern.

riskAssessment.risk: Double curl3 evaluation per vertex on low-tier GPUs
riskAssessment.planMitigation: Adequate — skipping turbulence pass for low noiseOctaves is the right approach. curl3 internally calls fbm3 which already respects octave count.
riskAssessment.additionalNotes: particleWarp.vert.glsl already uses curl3 without reported issues, so single curl3 is proven safe. The second (turbulence) pass is the marginal cost.

riskAssessment.risk: gl_PointSize hardware clamp on elongated sprites
riskAssessment.planMitigation: Adequate — clamping to 64.0 and relying on fragment ellipse falloff is the correct approach. Existing shaders already use gl_PointSize with similar scaling.
Adequate — minimum spacing check and adaptive source count are reasonable. The seeded PRNG pattern from ribbonField.ts is proven.

riskAssessment.risk: Mode rotation timing — adding a 7th mode reduces each mode's screen time
riskAssessment.planMitigation: Not addressed, but not a concern — mode rotation is 90-180s per mode, a 7th mode simply extends the full cycle from ~10-15min to ~11-17min.

US-041.reason: Provides noise3d.glsl with curl3 function used for flow-field displacement

US-048.reason: Provides shader error capture and health gate validation used in step 7

complete: A new vertex shader displaces points along curl noise flow-field lines
Points are rendered as elongated sprites aligned to their velocity direction
Flow speed is modulated by bass energy
Flow turbulence is modulated by treble energy
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors
Can be assigned to particleField or pointCloud systems
Visual character is distinctly different from existing warp shaders

suggestions.description: Reuse uDisplacementScale instead of introducing uFlowScale to maintain uniform set consistency across all systems. If distinct semantics are needed, add uFlowScale to COMMON_UNIFORMS so all systems share the same uniform block.

Consider exposing the flowRibbonField's internal Points object positions for constellation line attachment, matching ribbonField's readonly positions/pointCount pattern. This ensures the constellation overlay works with the new mode.

The plan's step 4 config interface includes enableSparkle but no sparkle logic is described in the vertex shader algorithm. Either add sparkle (snoise-based treble shimmer, matching ribbonWarp) or remove the config flag to avoid dead config.
